label: Social Cost of Greenhouse Gases (SC-GHG)

sc_co2.description: Social Cost of Carbon Dioxide. Represents the marginal damage from an additional tonne of CO2 emissions. EPA 2023 estimates using updated climate science and damage functions.

at_2_percent.label: Central estimate (2% near-term Ramsey discount rate)

at_1_5_percent.label: Low discount rate sensitivity (1.5% near-term Ramsey)

at_2_5_percent.label: High discount rate sensitivity (2.5% near-term Ramsey)

sc_ch4.description: Social Cost of Methane. Per tonne CH4.

sc_n2o.description: Social Cost of Nitrous Oxide. Per tonne N2O.

recommended_usage: Use sc_co2.at_2_percent as the central estimate, consistent with the revised A-4 discount rate. Present sensitivity at 1.5% and 2.5% rates. Always present all three discount rate scenarios.
current_status: The Interagency Working Group (IWG) on SC-GHG was disbanded by executive order in January 2025. Agencies were directed to stop factoring SC-GHG into rulemaking except where statute requires. However, the EPA 2023 technical report remains the most recent peer-reviewed estimate. For CBA purposes, these values represent the best available science on climate damages.

source.publication: Report on the Social Cost of Greenhouse Gases: Estimates Incorporating Recent Scientific Advances
source.update_frequency: periodic (no scheduled update due to IWG disbandment)

caveats: SC-GHG values are damage-based (social cost), unlike UK DESNZ values which are target-consistent (abatement cost). Not directly comparable.
The 2023 EPA estimates are significantly higher than the 2016 IWG estimates (which used SC-CO2 ~$51/t at 3%).
IWG disbanded Jan 2025. Regulatory applicability depends on agency-specific statutory authority.
Values are global damages, not US-only. Some agencies may prefer domestic-only SC-CO2 (roughly 10-23% of global).
Monte Carlo uncertainty ranges are wide. The 95th percentile at 2% discount rate exceeds $500/tCO2 for 2020.
